questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about surgical anatomy of the colon and rectum are correct?

answers.text: The cecum has the largest inner diameter of all segments of the colon (13 to 15 cm.).
The rectosigmoid junction is situated at approximately 15 to 18 cm. from the anus.
The rectum is entirely an intraperitoneal organ.
The ileocolic, right colic, and middle colic arteries are branches from the inferior mesenteric
The arterial arcade created by communicating vessels at 1 to 2 cm from the mesenteric is called the artery of Drummond.

questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about surgical procedures on the colon and rectum are correct?

answers.text: Successful healing of colonic anastomoses depends on the adequacy of the blood supply.
In excising part of the colon containing cancer, the lymphatics should be avoided by dividing the mesentery close to the wall of the colon.
Despite complete removal of the colon and rectum, transanal fecal flow can be preserved by means of an ileal pouch–anal anastomosis.
When a colostomy is created it cannot be reversed.
Colostomy can be life saving in patients with colonic perforation or obstruction.

questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about colon physiology are correct?

answers.text: Colonic recycling of urea is accomplished by the splitting of urea by bacterial ureases.
Fermentation by colonic bacteria may rescue malabsorbed carbohydrates.
The preferred fuel of the colonic epithelium is glucose.
Absorption by the colonic mucosa is a passive process.
Insoluble fibers create bulk in the stool.

questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about diagnostic studies for the colon and rectum are true?

answers.text: Acetylcholinesterase staining of rectal biopsies is unreliable for the diagnosis of Hirschsprung's disease.
Cinedefecography is useful for detecting “hidden” prolapse or rectal intussusception.
A negative osmotic gap in stool is indicative of secretory diarrhea.
A colonic transit time study involves serial abdominal x-rays after ingestion of radiopaque markers.
Carcinoembryonic antigen (CEA) is useful for monitoring patients after resection for colon cancer.

questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about the microbiology of the colon are true?

answers.text: The colon contains no more bacteria than the stomach.
The predominant bacteria in the colon are aerobic.
Nearly one third of the dry weight of feces is bacteria.
Common bacteria in the colon are Bacteroides, Bifidobacterium, and Enterobacterium species.
The colonic microflora is relatively stable.

questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about bowel preparation for colon surgery are true?

answers.text: Bowel preparation is accomplished by a combination of mechanical cleansing and nonabsorbable antibiotics.
Three days of clear liquids provides sufficient mechanical cleansing.
Commercial electrolyte-polyethylene glycol solutions provide mechanical cleansing without inducing electrolyte imbalance.
Non-absorbable antibiotics such as neomycin and erythromycin base are administered the day before the operation in three doses.
Intravenous antibiotics are also administered the day before surgery.

questions.question_text: The test with the highest diagnostic yield for detecting a colovesical fistula is:
Which of the following about diverticular disease is not true:

answers.text: It is more common in the United States and Western Europe than in Asia and Africa.
A low-fiber diet may predispose to development of diverticulosis.
It involves sigmoid colon in more than 90% of patients.
Eighty percent of general people develop diverticulitis sometime during their lifetime.
It is the most common cause of massive lower gastrointestinal hemorrhage.

questions.question_text: The most common indication for surgery secondary to acute diverticulitis is:
Which of the following statements about colorectal polyps are true?

answers.text: Familial juvenile polyposis is associated with an increased incidence of colon cancer.
Although the propensity for development of malignancy is related to the size of a neoplastic polyp, those with mixed tubule-villous histologic appearance are most likely to develop malignant changes.
The loss of a single tumor suppressor gene such as p53 is sufficient to lead to the development of malignancy in colorectal neoplastic polyps.
Endoscopic polypectomy results in a decreased incidence of carcinomas of the colon and rectum.

questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about familial adenomatous polyposis (FAP) is true?

answers.text: Inherited in an autosomal-dominant manner, this genetic defect is of variable penetrance, some patients having only a few polyps whereas others develop thousands.
The phenotypic expression of the disease depends mostly on the genotype.
Appropriate surgical therapy includes total abdominal colectomy with ileorectal anastomosis and ileoanal pull-through with rectal mucosectomy.
Panproctocolectomy with ileostomy is not appropriate therapy for this disease.
Pharmacologic management of this disease may be appropriate in some instances.

questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about the etiology of chronic ulcerative colitis are true?

answers.text: Ulcerative colitis is 50% less frequent in nonwhite than in white populations.
Psychosomatic factors play a major causative role in the development of ulcerative colitis.
Cytokines are integrally involved in the pathogenesis of ulcerative colitis.
Ulcerative colitis has been identified with a greater frequency in family members of patients with confirmed inflammatory bowel disease.
Ulcerative colitis is two to four times more common in Jewish than in non-Jewish populations.

questions.question_text: Surgical alternatives for the treatment of ulcerative colitis include all of the following, except:

answers.text: Proctocolectomy with Brooke ileostomy or continent ileostomy.
Subtotal colectomy with ileostomy and Hartmann closure of the rectum.

questions.question_text: The initial management of toxic ulcerative colitis should include:
Which finding suggest the diagnosis of chronic ulcerative colitis as opposed to Crohn's colitis?
Which of the following approaches are currently acceptable for the management of spontaneous pneumothorax?

answers.text: Chest tube replacement alone for the patient with a first episode.
Operation on presentation for any patient with a first episode.
Video-assisted thoracic surgery (VATS) bleb excision and pleurodesis for recurrent pneumothorax on the same side.
Thoracotomy with bleb excision and pleurodesis for unilateral recurrent pneumothorax.
Operation after a first episode in an airline pilot.

questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about lung abscess are true?

answers.text: Pyogenic lung abscess occurs most frequently in the lower lobe of the left lung.
Anaerobic bacteria are commonly present in pyogenic lung abscess.
Operation is usually required to eradicate a pyogenic lung abscess.
Penicillin is the treatment of choice for lung abscess.
Pyogenic lung abscess occurs only after blunt trauma of the chest.

questions.question_text: Which of the following correctly describe a patient with spontaneous pneumothorax?

answers.text: The patient is almost always elderly and debilitated.
An unsuspected primary or metastatic lung tumor may be present.
The administration of supplemental oxygen is of little benefit to the patient.
The patient should always be treated with an intercostal tube and closed pleural drainage.
Video-assisted thoracic surgery (VATS) should be considered for persistent air leak in patients with secondary spontaneous pneumothorax.

questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about spontaneous pneumothorax (PSP) are correct?

answers.text: The risk of recurrence after resolution of the first episode of PSP or secondary spontaneous pneumothorax (SSP) is 35% to 45%.
Patients with PSP are typically tall, thin, young adult males with a history of smoking.
Secondary spontaneous pneumothorax is associated with family history in 10% of cases.
For bleb resection and pleurodesis thoracoscopic thoracotomy and open thoracotomy provide similar cure rates for patients with primary spontaneous pneumothorax.
Causes of secondary pneumothorax include trauma and iatrogenic needle puncture.

questions.question_text: Which of the following would be the least appropriate in the management of acute suppurative mediastinitis?
Each of the following is appropriate for managing acute suppurative mediastinitis except:
The principal etiologic factor in lung abcess and gangene is:
Emergency treatment of lung edema consists in:

answers.text: Oxygenotherapy with positive expiratory pressure 10-15 cm H2O.
1/3 of dose of digoxyn for digitalisation (if not yet digitalisated and cardiac insufficiency is

questions.question_text: Wich microflora more frequently provokes developing of pulmonary gangrene?
The most frequent localization of aspiration lung abcesses is:

answers.text: Basal anterior segments of inferior lobes.
Posterior segments of lung /predominantly superior lobes/.

questions.question_text: The causes of chronisation of acute empyema are:

answers.text: Agresive and resistant to antibiotics microflora.

questions.question_text: Which affirmations regarding pulmonary anatomy are correct?

answers.text: Right lung has the middle lobe.

questions.question_text: Segments of the lobes of the lung are following:

answers.text: Superior lobe of right lung consists of 3 segments /1,2,3/.

questions.question_text: Hemoptysis may occure in following situations:
Hemostasis in pulmonary gangrene with repeated lung hemorrhage is performed by:
The bronchial wall includes the following layers:
Which of the following respiratory volumes are correct?
Which of the following would be the least appropriate in the management of acute suppurative mediastinitis?
Each of the following is appropriate for managing acute suppurative mediastinitis, except:
Clinical features suggestive of myasthenia gravis include all of the following except:
When progressive enlargement of a multinodular goiter causes symptomatic tracheal compression, the preferred management in otherwise good-risk patients is:

answers.text: Surgical resection of the abnormal thyroid.

questions.question_text: The most precise diagnostic screening procedure for differentiating benign thyroid nodules from malignant is:
Which is the preferred operation for initial management of a thyroid nodule, that is considered suspicious for malignancy by fine-needle-aspiration biopsy (FNAB):
All of the following are components of the MEN type 2B syndrome, except:

answers.text: Multiple neuromas on the lips, tongue, and oral mucosa.

questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about hypoparathyroidism are true:

answers.text: Is most commonly encountered as a postviral syndrome.
Can be associated with marked hypocalcemia after parathyroidectomy in patients with bone disease.
Can cause anxiety, depression, or confusion.
Can cause physical signs such as Chvostek's and Trousseau's signs.
Is treatable acutely with intravenous calcium salts and chronically with oral calcium and

questions.question_text: Which steatment about Addisonian crisis, or acute adrenocortical insufficiency, is true:

answers.text: Occurs only in patients with known adrenal insufficiency or in those receiving long-term supraphysiologic doses of exogenous steroids.
Can mimic an acute abdomen with fever, nausea and vomiting, abdominal pain, and hypotension.
May cause electrolyte abnormalities, including hypernatremia, hypokalemia, hypoglycemia, and hypercalcemia, as well as eosinophilia on peripheral blood smear.
Should be diagnosed with the rapid ACTH stimulation test before steroid replacement is instituted.
May be effectively treated with intravenous “stress-dose” glucocorticoid and mineralocorticoid replacement.

questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about the synthesis of thyroid hormone and its physiology are true?

answers.text: The iodine utilized in hormone synthesis is derived principally from dietary sources.
The role of thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH) in thyroid physiology is limited to regulation of the release of thyroid hormone in plasma.
Enough thyroxine (T4) is stored in the normal thyroid to provide a euthyroid state for 3 weeks despite absence of iodine intake.
The regulation of thyroid function involves pituitary, but not hypothalamic, input.
Thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH) is produced by suprarenal gland in stress situations.

questions.question_text: Hyperthyroidism can be caused by all of the following pathologies, except:
The most common cause of goitrous hypothyroidism in adults is:
Which of the following are surgical indications for thyroidectomy in Grave’s disease?
Which of the following is the principal blood supply to the parathyroid glands?

answers.text: Parathyroid arterial branches directly from the external carotid artery.

questions.question_text: Which of the following pharmacologic agents can be used in the treatment of thyrotoxicosis to block the production of thyroid hormone?
Which of the following statements regarding fine needle aspiration cytology of a thyroid nodule are true?

answers.text: It differentiates neoplastic and non-neoplastic nodules in most cases.
It does not allow differentiation of papillary, medullary and anaplastic carcinoma.
It cannot differentiate malignant and benign follicular or Hürthle cell neoplasms.
It is not recommended when a patient has a history of head and neck radiation.
It is high recommended in cysts of the neck.

questions.question_text: Hyperthyroidism results from all of the conditions noted below. Which of the following commonly require surgical management?
Which of the following statements regarding thyroid physiology are true?

answers.text: Normally about 20% of T3 is secreted directly from the thyroid gland.
The thyroid gland is the only endogenous source of T4.
Excess thyroid hormone results in an increase in the number of ATP-dependent sodium pumps on the cell membrane.
The majority of thyroid hormone in circulating plasma is bound to albumin, thus limiting the availability of the metabolically active form.
Thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH) decrease the levels of T3 and T4 in blood stream.

questions.question_text: Which is the definitive treatment of choice for toxic multinodular goiter?

answers.text: Unilateral total lobectomy on the side of dominant disease.

questions.question_text: Which of the following are causes of Cushing syndrome?
The goiter situated in mediastinum is considered as:
Exophtalmus at one’s patient with goiter is a result of the compression of:
Normal radioiodine uptake in 2 hours is:

Which of the following manifestations could be a result of overflow of thyroid hormones in peripheral blood:
All of the following are complications of peptic ulcer surgery, except:
The presentation of Zollinger-Ellison syndrome includes all of the following, except:

answers.text: Hyperparathyroidism in patients with multiple endocrine neoplasia type 1 (MEN 1) syndrome.

questions.question_text: All affirmations about the dumping syndrome are true, except:

answers.text: Symptoms can be controlled with a somatostatin analog.
Diarrhea is always part of the dumping syndrome.
Flushing and tachycardia are common features of the syndrome.
Separating solids and liquids in the patient's oral intake alleviates some of the symptoms of the syndrome.
Early postoperative dumping after vagotomy often resolves spontaneously.

questions.question_text: Which is the endoscopic finding, associated with the highest incidence of rebleeding in patients with bleeding duodenal ulcers:
All of the following measures have been recommended for control of acid secretion in patients with Zollinger-Ellison syndrome, except:
All of the following contribute to peptic ulcer disease, except:
Which are the major stimulants of acid secretion by the gastric parietal cells:
Which are the methods of examination, used for determining the cause of postoperative peptic ulcer?

answers.text: Tests to evaluate the gastric secretion.

questions.question_text: Which of the following approaches are included in the treatment of postoperative peptic ulcer:

answers.text: Excluding the remedies with toxic effect to gastrointestinal mucosa.

questions.question_text: Which of following clinical situations after stomach surgery sometimes need near total gastrectomy?
Which of the following surgical interventions are most effective in treatment of dumping syndrome:

answers.text: Reduodenisation with reduction of evacuation throu gastric stump.

questions.question_text: Which is the operation of choice in gastro-jejuno-colic fistulas:

answers.text: Triple resection with gastrojejunal and colo-colonic anasthomosis.

questions.question_text: First of all the frequency of postoperative peptic ulcer is determined by:

answers.text: Ulcer’s complications at the time of operation.

questions.question_text: After what type of operation for duodenal ulcer the appearance of postoperative peptic ulcer is less possible?
Fibroscopy reveals the third grade esophageal varices which bleed,the gastric mucosa without ulceration. Name the first method of hemostasis:

answers.text: The resection of inferior esophagus with gastroesophageal anasthomosis.
Plugging with Blackmore probe, intravenous pituitrine.

questions.question_text: What is the most safest and objective method of diagnosing upper digestive haemorrhage?
What is the most useful method in the treatment of cirrhous portal hypertension,?
What is the most common cause of portal hypertension?
Which gradation is usedto assess the liver function in cirrhotic patients with portal hypertension:
That is the indication for Tanner surgery in patients with portal hupertension:

answers.text: Haemorrhagy through rupture of esophageal varices.
Haemorrhagy through rupture of colonic varices.
Haemorrhagy through rupture of rectal varices.

questions.question_text: What is the wrong affirmation about the prehepatic form of portal hypertension?

answers.text: Distal spleno-renal anastomosis reduces portal pressure.
Is caused by the development of congenital anomalies of the portal vein.
Causes the onset of digestive haemorrhage.

questions.question_text: Cirrhogenic portal hypertension has the following complications,with the exception of:
List the causes of digestive bleeding in the context of portal hypertension:
List the major risks of using the Blackmore probe:
Which of the following surgeries does rarely lead to hepatic encephalopathy?
Which of the following therapeutic modalities can be used to stop bleeding from esophageal varices?
What are the goals of surgical treatment in portal hypertension?

answers.text: Control of acute gastroesophagial variceal hemorrhage.
Reducing the risk of relapse of variceal bleeding.

questions.question_text: The Child classification criteria for the assessment of liver function include:
Which of the following statements about the porto-caval shunt in emergency cases are true?

answers.text: Should be followed by liver transplantation.
A selective type shunt is preffered.

questions.question_text: The spleen response to portal hypertension, regardless of the obstacle site, is:

answers.text: Hypervascularization with increased portal pressure through hiperaflux.
The occurrence of arterio-venous lienal fistulas

questions.question_text: Which of the following conditions can cause a pre-hepatic portal obstruction?

answers.text: Phlegm of the shaft of portal vein.
Thrombosis of the spleno-portal venous spindle.

questions.question_text: Which of the conditions below may cause intraheptatic portal obstruction?
The main causes of the suprahepatic portal obstruction are?
Intermediate host for Echinococcus granulosus may be any of the one’s listed, except:
Complications of the Hydatid disease of the liver are the following, except:

answers.text: The rift in the pleura, bronchi.
The rupture in the abdominal cavity.
The rupture in the biliary pathways.

questions.question_text: What statement concerning the liver hydatid cyst is incorrect:

answers.text: It is most commonly located in the left lobe of the liver.
Serology is often positive for hydatidosis.
It may erupt in biliary ways, pleura, abdominal cavity.

questions.question_text: The most common benign tumor of the liver is represented by:
What statement concerning the hepatic alveolar echinococcosis is true:

answers.text: Ultrasound shows a single cystic image.
It is transmitted primarily through foxes.
It is often met especially in Moldova and southern Ukraine.
It is caused by Echinococcus granulosus.
Surgical treatment consists in the cysts liner.

questions.question_text: What statement,related to non parasitic liver cyst, is incorrect:

answers.text: Ecoguided punction is the optimal treatment solution.
Some complications may appear in evolution.

questions.question_text: The treatment of choice in hydatid cyst of liver is:
When is indicated marginal hepatic resection in liver hydatidosis:

answers.text: In all kinds of hydatid cysts whatever their evolutionary stage.
In suppurated hydatid cyst with liver piosclerosis.
In hepatic hydatid cyst complicated with biliary fistula.
In hepatic hydatid cyst located marginally.

questions.question_text: Which of the following statements,referring to the alveolar echinococcosis of the liver, are false:

answers.text: It has an invasive growth and a gloomy prognosis.
Optimal treatment solution is peritoneal lavage-puncture.
